packages.name: cobra
packages.synopsis: Package cobra is a commander providing a simple interface to create powerful modern CLI interfaces.
packages.doc:
Package cobra is a commander providing a simple interface to create powerful modern CLI interfaces.
In addition to providing an interface, Cobra simultaneously provides a controller to organize your application code.

consts.name: ShellCompRequestCmd
ShellCompNoDescRequestCmd
ShellCompDirectiveError
ShellCompDirectiveNoSpace
ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp
ShellCompDirectiveFilterFileExt
ShellCompDirectiveFilterDirs
ShellCompDirectiveKeepOrder
ShellCompDirectiveDefault

vars.name: EnableCaseInsensitive
vars.doc: EnableCaseInsensitive allows case-insensitive commands names. (case sensitive by default)

vars.name: EnableCommandSorting
vars.doc:
EnableCommandSorting controls sorting of the slice of commands, which is turned on by default.
To disable sorting, set it to false.

vars.name: EnablePrefixMatching
vars.doc:
EnablePrefixMatching allows setting automatic prefix matching. Automatic prefix matching can be a dangerous thing
to automatically enable in CLI tools.
Set this to true to enable it.

vars.name: EnableTraverseRunHooks
vars.doc:
EnableTraverseRunHooks executes persistent pre-run and post-run hooks from all parents.
By default this is disabled, which means only the first run hook to be found is executed.

vars.name: MousetrapDisplayDuration
vars.doc:
MousetrapDisplayDuration controls how long the MousetrapHelpText message is displayed on Windows
if the CLI is started from explorer.exe. Set to 0 to wait for the return key to be pressed.
To disable the mousetrap, just set MousetrapHelpText to blank string ("").
Works only on Microsoft Windows.

vars.name: MousetrapHelpText
vars.doc:
MousetrapHelpText enables an information splash screen on Windows
if the CLI is started from explorer.exe.
To disable the mousetrap, just set this variable to blank string ("").
Works only on Microsoft Windows.
vars.declaration:
var MousetrapHelpText = `This is a command line tool.

You need to open cmd.exe and run it from there.
`

funcs.name: AddTemplateFunc
funcs.doc:
AddTemplateFunc adds a template function that's available to Usage and Help
template generation.

funcs.name: AddTemplateFuncs
funcs.doc:
AddTemplateFuncs adds multiple template functions that are available to Usage and
Help template generation.

funcs.name: AppendActiveHelp
funcs.doc:
AppendActiveHelp adds the specified string to the specified array to be used as ActiveHelp.
Such strings will be processed by the completion script and will be shown as ActiveHelp
to the user.
The array parameter should be the array that will contain the completions.
This function can be called multiple times before and/or after completions are added to
the array.  Each time this function is called with the same array, the new
ActiveHelp line will be shown below the previous ones when completion is triggered.

funcs.name: ArbitraryArgs
funcs.doc: ArbitraryArgs never returns an error.

funcs.name: CheckErr
funcs.doc: CheckErr prints the msg with the prefix 'Error:' and exits with error code 1. If the msg is nil, it does nothing.

funcs.name: CompDebug
funcs.doc:
CompDebug prints the specified string to the same file as where the
completion script prints its logs.
Note that completion printouts should never be on stdout as they would
be wrongly interpreted as actual completion choices by the completion script.

funcs.name: CompDebugln
funcs.doc:
CompDebugln prints the specified string with a newline at the end
to the same file as where the completion script prints its logs.
Such logs are only printed when the user has set the environment
variable BASH_COMP_DEBUG_FILE to the path of some file to be used.

funcs.name: CompError
funcs.doc: CompError prints the specified completion message to stderr.

funcs.name: CompErrorln
funcs.doc: CompErrorln prints the specified completion message to stderr with a newline at the end.

funcs.name: CompletionWithDesc
funcs.doc: CompletionWithDesc returns a [Completion] with a description by using the TAB delimited format.
funcs.signature: func CompletionWithDesc(choice string, description string) Completion

funcs.name: Eq
funcs.doc: Eq takes two types and checks whether they are equal. Supported types are int and string. Unsupported types will panic.
funcs.signature: func Eq(a interface{}, b interface{}) bool

funcs.name: ExactArgs
funcs.doc: ExactArgs returns an error if there are not exactly n args.

funcs.name: ExactValidArgs
funcs.doc:
ExactValidArgs returns an error if there are not exactly N positional args OR
there are any positional args that are not in the `ValidArgs` field of `Command`

Deprecated: use MatchAll(ExactArgs(n), OnlyValidArgs) instead

funcs.name: FixedCompletions
funcs.doc:
FixedCompletions can be used to create a completion function which always
returns the same results.

This method returns a function that satisfies [CompletionFunc]
It can be used with [Command.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc] and for [Command.ValidArgsFunction].

funcs.name: GetActiveHelpConfig
funcs.doc:
GetActiveHelpConfig returns the value of the ActiveHelp environment variable
<PROGRAM>_ACTIVE_HELP where <PROGRAM> is the name of the root command in upper
case, with all non-ASCII-alphanumeric characters replaced by `_`.
It will always return "0" if the global environment variable COBRA_ACTIVE_HELP
is set to "0".

funcs.name: Gt
funcs.doc:
Gt takes two types and checks whether the first type is greater than the second. In case of types Arrays, Chans,
Maps and Slices, Gt will compare their lengths. Ints are compared directly while strings are first parsed as
ints and then compared.
funcs.signature: func Gt(a interface{}, b interface{}) bool

funcs.name: MarkFlagCustom
funcs.doc:
MarkFlagCustom adds the BashCompCustom annotation to the named flag, if it exists.
The bash completion script will call the bash function f for the flag.

This will only work for bash completion.
It is recommended to instead use c.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc(...) which allows
to register a Go function which will work across all shells.
funcs.signature: func MarkFlagCustom(flags *pflag.FlagSet, name string, f string) error

funcs.name: MarkFlagDirname
funcs.doc:
MarkFlagDirname instructs the various shell completion implementations to
limit completions for the named flag to directory names.

funcs.name: MarkFlagFilename
funcs.doc:
MarkFlagFilename instructs the various shell completion implementations to
limit completions for the named flag to the specified file extensions.
funcs.signature: func MarkFlagFilename(flags *pflag.FlagSet, name string, extensions ...string) error

funcs.name: MarkFlagRequired
funcs.doc:
MarkFlagRequired instructs the various shell completion implementations to
prioritize the named flag when performing completion,
and causes your command to report an error if invoked without the flag.

funcs.name: MatchAll
funcs.doc: MatchAll allows combining several PositionalArgs to work in concert.

funcs.name: MaximumNArgs
funcs.doc: MaximumNArgs returns an error if there are more than N args.

funcs.name: MinimumNArgs
funcs.doc: MinimumNArgs returns an error if there is not at least N args.

funcs.name: NoArgs
funcs.doc: NoArgs returns an error if any args are included.

funcs.name: NoDuplicateArgs
funcs.doc: NoDuplicateArgs returns an error if there are any duplicate positional args.

funcs.name: NoFileCompletions
funcs.doc:
NoFileCompletions can be used to disable file completion for commands that should
not trigger file completions.

This method satisfies [CompletionFunc].
It can be used with [Command.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc] and for [Command.ValidArgsFunction].
funcs.signature: func NoFileCompletions(cmd *Command, args []string, toComplete string) ([]Completion, ShellCompDirective)

funcs.name: OnFinalize
funcs.doc:
OnFinalize sets the passed functions to be run when each command's
Execute method is terminated.

funcs.name: OnInitialize
funcs.doc:
OnInitialize sets the passed functions to be run when each command's
Execute method is called.

funcs.name: OnlyValidArgs
funcs.doc:
OnlyValidArgs returns an error if there are any positional args that are not in
the `ValidArgs` field of `Command`

funcs.name: RangeArgs
funcs.doc: RangeArgs returns an error if the number of args is not within the expected range.
funcs.signature: func RangeArgs(min int, max int) PositionalArgs

funcs.name: WriteStringAndCheck
funcs.doc: WriteStringAndCheck writes a string into a buffer, and checks if the error is not nil.

types.name: Command
types.doc:
Command is just that, a command for your application.
E.g.  'go run ...' - 'run' is the command. Cobra requires
you to define the usage and description as part of your command
definition to ensure usability.

fields.name: Use
fields.doc:
Use is the one-line usage message.
Recommended syntax is as follows:
  [ ] identifies an optional argument. Arguments that are not enclosed in brackets are required.
  ... indicates that you can specify multiple values for the previous argument.
  |   indicates mutually exclusive information. You can use the argument to the left of the separator or the
      argument to the right of the separator. You cannot use both arguments in a single use of the command.
  { } delimits a set of mutually exclusive arguments when one of the arguments is required. If the arguments are
      optional, they are enclosed in brackets ([ ]).
Example: add [-F file | -D dir]... [-f format] profile

fields.name: Aliases
fields.doc: Aliases is an array of aliases that can be used instead of the first word in Use.

fields.name: SuggestFor
fields.doc:
SuggestFor is an array of command names for which this command will be suggested -
similar to aliases but only suggests.

fields.name: Short
fields.doc: Short is the short description shown in the 'help' output.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.doc: The group id under which this subcommand is grouped in the 'help' output of its parent.

fields.name: Long
fields.doc: Long is the long message shown in the 'help <this-command>' output.

fields.name: Example
fields.doc: Example is examples of how to use the command.

fields.name: ValidArgs
fields.doc: ValidArgs is list of all valid non-flag arguments that are accepted in shell completions

fields.name: ValidArgsFunction
fields.doc:
ValidArgsFunction is an optional function that provides valid non-flag arguments for shell completion.
It is a dynamic version of using ValidArgs.
Only one of ValidArgs and ValidArgsFunction can be used for a command.

fields.name: Args
fields.doc: Expected arguments

fields.name: ArgAliases
fields.doc:
ArgAliases is List of aliases for ValidArgs.
These are not suggested to the user in the shell completion,
but accepted if entered manually.

fields.name: BashCompletionFunction
fields.doc:
BashCompletionFunction is custom bash functions used by the legacy bash autocompletion generator.
For portability with other shells, it is recommended to instead use ValidArgsFunction

fields.name: Deprecated
fields.doc: Deprecated defines, if this command is deprecated and should print this string when used.

fields.name: Annotations
fields.doc:
Annotations are key/value pairs that can be used by applications to identify or
group commands or set special options.

fields.name: Version
fields.doc:
Version defines the version for this command. If this value is non-empty and the command does not
define a "version" flag, a "version" boolean flag will be added to the command and, if specified,
will print content of the "Version" variable. A shorthand "v" flag will also be added if the
command does not define one.

fields.name: PersistentPreRun
fields.doc:
The *Run functions are executed in the following order:
  * PersistentPreRun()
  * PreRun()
  * Run()
  * PostRun()
  * PersistentPostRun()
All functions get the same args, the arguments after the command name.
The *PreRun and *PostRun functions will only be executed if the Run function of the current
command has been declared.

PersistentPreRun: children of this command will inherit and execute.

fields.name: PersistentPreRunE
fields.doc: PersistentPreRunE: PersistentPreRun but returns an error.

fields.name: PreRun
fields.doc: PreRun: children of this command will not inherit.

fields.name: PreRunE
fields.doc: PreRunE: PreRun but returns an error.

fields.name: Run
fields.doc: Run: Typically the actual work function. Most commands will only implement this.

fields.name: RunE
fields.doc: RunE: Run but returns an error.

fields.name: PostRun
fields.doc: PostRun: run after the Run command.

fields.name: PostRunE
fields.doc: PostRunE: PostRun but returns an error.

fields.name: PersistentPostRun
fields.doc: PersistentPostRun: children of this command will inherit and execute after PostRun.

fields.name: PersistentPostRunE
fields.doc: PersistentPostRunE: PersistentPostRun but returns an error.

fields.name: FParseErrWhitelist
fields.doc: FParseErrWhitelist flag parse errors to be ignored

fields.name: CompletionOptions
fields.doc: CompletionOptions is a set of options to control the handling of shell completion

fields.name: TraverseChildren
fields.doc: TraverseChildren parses flags on all parents before executing child command.

fields.name: Hidden
fields.doc: Hidden defines, if this command is hidden and should NOT show up in the list of available commands.

fields.name: SilenceErrors
fields.doc: SilenceErrors is an option to quiet errors down stream.

fields.name: SilenceUsage
fields.doc: SilenceUsage is an option to silence usage when an error occurs.

fields.name: DisableFlagParsing
fields.doc:
DisableFlagParsing disables the flag parsing.
If this is true all flags will be passed to the command as arguments.

fields.name: DisableAutoGenTag
fields.doc:
DisableAutoGenTag defines, if gen tag ("Auto generated by spf13/cobra...")
will be printed by generating docs for this command.

fields.name: DisableFlagsInUseLine
fields.doc:
DisableFlagsInUseLine will disable the addition of [flags] to the usage
line of a command when printing help or generating docs

fields.name: DisableSuggestions
fields.doc:
DisableSuggestions disables the suggestions based on Levenshtein distance
that go along with 'unknown command' messages.

fields.name: SuggestionsMinimumDistance
fields.doc:
SuggestionsMinimumDistance defines minimum levenshtein distance to display suggestions.
Must be > 0.

methods.name: AddCommand
methods.doc: AddCommand adds one or more commands to this parent command.

methods.name: AddGroup
methods.doc: AddGroup adds one or more command groups to this parent command.

methods.name: AllChildCommandsHaveGroup
methods.doc: AllChildCommandsHaveGroup returns if all subcommands are assigned to a group

methods.name: ArgsLenAtDash
methods.doc:
ArgsLenAtDash will return the length of c.Flags().Args at the moment
when a -- was found during args parsing.

methods.name: CalledAs
methods.doc:
CalledAs returns the command name or alias that was used to invoke
this command or an empty string if the command has not been called.

methods.name: CommandPath
methods.doc: CommandPath returns the full path to this command.

methods.name: CommandPathPadding
methods.doc: CommandPathPadding return padding for the command path.

methods.name: Commands
methods.doc: Commands returns a sorted slice of child commands.

methods.name: ContainsGroup
methods.doc: ContainsGroup return if groupID exists in the list of command groups.

methods.name: Context
methods.doc:
Context returns underlying command context. If command was executed
with ExecuteContext or the context was set with SetContext, the
previously set context will be returned. Otherwise, nil is returned.

Notice that a call to Execute and ExecuteC will replace a nil context of
a command with a context.Background, so a background context will be
returned by Context after one of these functions has been called.

methods.name: DebugFlags
methods.doc:
DebugFlags used to determine which flags have been assigned to which commands
and which persist.

methods.name: DisplayName
methods.doc:
DisplayName returns the name to display in help text. Returns command Name()
If CommandDisplayNameAnnoation is not set

methods.name: ErrOrStderr
methods.doc: ErrOrStderr returns output to stderr

methods.name: ErrPrefix
methods.doc: ErrPrefix return error message prefix for the command

methods.name: Execute
methods.doc:
Execute uses the args (os.Args[1:] by default)
and run through the command tree finding appropriate matches
for commands and then corresponding flags.

methods.name: ExecuteC
methods.doc: ExecuteC executes the command.

methods.name: ExecuteContext
methods.doc:
ExecuteContext is the same as Execute(), but sets the ctx on the command.
Retrieve ctx by calling cmd.Context() inside your *Run lifecycle or ValidArgs
functions.

methods.name: ExecuteContextC
methods.doc:
ExecuteContextC is the same as ExecuteC(), but sets the ctx on the command.
Retrieve ctx by calling cmd.Context() inside your *Run lifecycle or ValidArgs
functions.

methods.name: Find
methods.doc:
Find the target command given the args and command tree
Meant to be run on the highest node. Only searches down.

methods.name: Flag
methods.doc: Flag climbs up the command tree looking for matching flag.

methods.name: FlagErrorFunc
methods.doc:
FlagErrorFunc returns either the function set by SetFlagErrorFunc for this
command or a parent, or it returns a function which returns the original
error.

methods.name: Flags
methods.doc:
Flags returns the complete FlagSet that applies
to this command (local and persistent declared here and by all parents).

methods.name: GenBashCompletion
methods.doc: GenBashCompletion generates bash completion file and writes to the passed writer.

methods.name: GenBashCompletionFile
methods.doc: GenBashCompletionFile generates bash completion file.

methods.name: GenBashCompletionFileV2
methods.doc: GenBashCompletionFileV2 generates Bash completion version 2.
methods.signature: func  GenBashCompletionFileV2(filename string, includeDesc bool) error

methods.name: GenBashCompletionV2
methods.doc:
GenBashCompletionV2 generates Bash completion file version 2
and writes it to the passed writer.
methods.signature: func  GenBashCompletionV2(w io.Writer, includeDesc bool) error

methods.name: GenFishCompletion
methods.doc: GenFishCompletion generates fish completion file and writes to the passed writer.
methods.signature: func  GenFishCompletion(w io.Writer, includeDesc bool) error

methods.name: GenFishCompletionFile
methods.doc: GenFishCompletionFile generates fish completion file.
methods.signature: func  GenFishCompletionFile(filename string, includeDesc bool) error

methods.name: GenPowerShellCompletion
methods.doc:
GenPowerShellCompletion generates powershell completion file without descriptions
and writes it to the passed writer.

methods.name: GenPowerShellCompletionFile
methods.doc: GenPowerShellCompletionFile generates powershell completion file without descriptions.

methods.name: GenPowerShellCompletionFileWithDesc
methods.doc: GenPowerShellCompletionFileWithDesc generates powershell completion file with descriptions.

methods.name: GenPowerShellCompletionWithDesc
methods.doc:
GenPowerShellCompletionWithDesc generates powershell completion file with descriptions
and writes it to the passed writer.

methods.name: GenZshCompletion
methods.doc:
GenZshCompletion generates zsh completion file including descriptions
and writes it to the passed writer.

methods.name: GenZshCompletionFile
methods.doc: GenZshCompletionFile generates zsh completion file including descriptions.

methods.name: GenZshCompletionFileNoDesc
methods.doc: GenZshCompletionFileNoDesc generates zsh completion file without descriptions.

methods.name: GenZshCompletionNoDesc
methods.doc:
GenZshCompletionNoDesc generates zsh completion file without descriptions
and writes it to the passed writer.

methods.name: GetFlagCompletionFunc
methods.doc: GetFlagCompletionFunc returns the completion function for the given flag of the command, if available.

methods.name: GlobalNormalizationFunc
methods.doc: GlobalNormalizationFunc returns the global normalization function or nil if it doesn't exist.
methods.signature: func  GlobalNormalizationFunc() func(f *flag.FlagSet, name string) flag.NormalizedName

methods.name: Groups
methods.doc: Groups returns a slice of child command groups.

methods.name: HasAlias
methods.doc: HasAlias determines if a given string is an alias of the command.

methods.name: HasAvailableFlags
methods.doc:
HasAvailableFlags checks if the command contains any flags (local plus persistent from the entire
structure) which are not hidden or deprecated.

methods.name: HasAvailableInheritedFlags
methods.doc:
HasAvailableInheritedFlags checks if the command has flags inherited from its parent command which are
not hidden or deprecated.

methods.name: HasAvailableLocalFlags
methods.doc:
HasAvailableLocalFlags checks if the command has flags specifically declared locally which are not hidden
or deprecated.

methods.name: HasAvailablePersistentFlags
methods.doc: HasAvailablePersistentFlags checks if the command contains persistent flags which are not hidden or deprecated.

methods.name: HasAvailableSubCommands
methods.doc:
HasAvailableSubCommands determines if a command has available sub commands that
need to be shown in the usage/help default template under 'available commands'.

methods.name: HasExample
methods.doc: HasExample determines if the command has example.

methods.name: HasFlags
methods.doc: HasFlags checks if the command contains any flags (local plus persistent from the entire structure).

methods.name: HasHelpSubCommands
methods.doc:
HasHelpSubCommands determines if a command has any available 'help' sub commands
that need to be shown in the usage/help default template under 'additional help
topics'.

methods.name: HasInheritedFlags
methods.doc: HasInheritedFlags checks if the command has flags inherited from its parent command.

methods.name: HasLocalFlags
methods.doc: HasLocalFlags checks if the command has flags specifically declared locally.

methods.name: HasParent
methods.doc: HasParent determines if the command is a child command.

methods.name: HasPersistentFlags
methods.doc: HasPersistentFlags checks if the command contains persistent flags.

methods.name: HasSubCommands
methods.doc: HasSubCommands determines if the command has children commands.

methods.name: Help
methods.doc:
Help puts out the help for the command.
Used when a user calls help [command].
Can be defined by user by overriding HelpFunc.

methods.name: HelpFunc
methods.doc:
HelpFunc returns either the function set by SetHelpFunc for this command
or a parent, or it returns a function with default help behavior.

methods.name: HelpTemplate
methods.doc:
HelpTemplate return help template for the command.
This function is kept for backwards-compatibility reasons.

methods.name: InOrStdin
methods.doc: InOrStdin returns input to stdin

methods.name: InheritedFlags
methods.doc:
InheritedFlags returns all flags which were inherited from parent commands.
This function does not modify the flags of the current command, it's purpose is to return the current state.

methods.name: InitDefaultCompletionCmd
methods.doc:
InitDefaultCompletionCmd adds a default 'completion' command to c.
This function will do nothing if any of the following is true:
1- the feature has been explicitly disabled by the program,
2- c has no subcommands (to avoid creating one),
3- c already has a 'completion' command provided by the program.

methods.name: InitDefaultHelpCmd
methods.doc:
InitDefaultHelpCmd adds default help command to c.
It is called automatically by executing the c or by calling help and usage.
If c already has help command or c has no subcommands, it will do nothing.

methods.name: InitDefaultHelpFlag
methods.doc:
InitDefaultHelpFlag adds default help flag to c.
It is called automatically by executing the c or by calling help and usage.
If c already has help flag, it will do nothing.

methods.name: InitDefaultVersionFlag
methods.doc:
InitDefaultVersionFlag adds default version flag to c.
It is called automatically by executing the c.
If c already has a version flag, it will do nothing.
If c.Version is empty, it will do nothing.

methods.name: IsAdditionalHelpTopicCommand
methods.doc:
IsAdditionalHelpTopicCommand determines if a command is an additional
help topic command; additional help topic command is determined by the
fact that it is NOT runnable/hidden/deprecated, and has no sub commands that
are runnable/hidden/deprecated.
Concrete example: https://github.com/spf13/cobra/issues/393#issuecomment-282741924.

methods.name: IsAvailableCommand
methods.doc:
IsAvailableCommand determines if a command is available as a non-help command
(this includes all non deprecated/hidden commands).

methods.name: LocalFlags
methods.doc:
LocalFlags returns the local FlagSet specifically set in the current command.
This function does not modify the flags of the current command, it's purpose is to return the current state.

methods.name: LocalNonPersistentFlags
methods.doc:
LocalNonPersistentFlags are flags specific to this command which will NOT persist to subcommands.
This function does not modify the flags of the current command, it's purpose is to return the current state.

methods.name: MarkFlagCustom
methods.doc:
MarkFlagCustom adds the BashCompCustom annotation to the named flag, if it exists.
The bash completion script will call the bash function f for the flag.

This will only work for bash completion.
It is recommended to instead use c.RegisterFlagCompletionFunc(...) which allows
to register a Go function which will work across all shells.
methods.signature: func  MarkFlagCustom(name string, f string) error

methods.name: MarkFlagDirname
methods.doc:
MarkFlagDirname instructs the various shell completion implementations to
limit completions for the named flag to directory names.

methods.name: MarkFlagFilename
methods.doc:
MarkFlagFilename instructs the various shell completion implementations to
limit completions for the named flag to the specified file extensions.

methods.name: MarkFlagRequired
methods.doc:
MarkFlagRequired instructs the various shell completion implementations to
prioritize the named flag when performing completion,
and causes your command to report an error if invoked without the flag.

methods.name: MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive
methods.doc:
MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive marks the given flags with annotations so that Cobra errors
if the command is invoked with more than one flag from the given set of flags.

methods.name: MarkFlagsOneRequired
methods.doc:
MarkFlagsOneRequired marks the given flags with annotations so that Cobra errors
if the command is invoked without at least one flag from the given set of flags.

methods.name: MarkFlagsRequiredTogether
methods.doc:
MarkFlagsRequiredTogether marks the given flags with annotations so that Cobra errors
if the command is invoked with a subset (but not all) of the given flags.

methods.name: MarkPersistentFlagDirname
methods.doc:
MarkPersistentFlagDirname instructs the various shell completion
implementations to limit completions for the named persistent flag to
directory names.

methods.name: MarkPersistentFlagFilename
methods.doc:
MarkPersistentFlagFilename instructs the various shell completion
implementations to limit completions for the named persistent flag to the
specified file extensions.

methods.name: MarkPersistentFlagRequired
methods.doc:
MarkPersistentFlagRequired instructs the various shell completion implementations to
prioritize the named persistent flag when performing completion,
and causes your command to report an error if invoked without the flag.

methods.name: MarkZshCompPositionalArgumentFile
methods.doc:
MarkZshCompPositionalArgumentFile only worked for zsh and its behavior was
not consistent with Bash completion. It has therefore been disabled.
Instead, when no other completion is specified, file completion is done by
default for every argument. One can disable file completion on a per-argument
basis by using ValidArgsFunction and ShellCompDirectiveNoFileComp.
To achieve file extension filtering, one can use ValidArgsFunction and
ShellCompDirectiveFilterFileExt.

Deprecated

methods.name: MarkZshCompPositionalArgumentWords
methods.doc:
MarkZshCompPositionalArgumentWords only worked for zsh. It has therefore
been disabled.
To achieve the same behavior across all shells, one can use
ValidArgs (for the first argument only) or ValidArgsFunction for
any argument (can include the first one also).

Deprecated

methods.name: Name
methods.doc: Name returns the command's name: the first word in the use line.

methods.name: NameAndAliases
methods.doc: NameAndAliases returns a list of the command name and all aliases

methods.name: NamePadding
methods.doc: NamePadding returns padding for the name.

methods.name: NonInheritedFlags
methods.doc:
NonInheritedFlags returns all flags which were not inherited from parent commands.
This function does not modify the flags of the current command, it's purpose is to return the current state.

methods.name: OutOrStderr
methods.doc: OutOrStderr returns output to stderr

methods.name: OutOrStdout
methods.doc: OutOrStdout returns output to stdout.

methods.name: Parent
methods.doc: Parent returns a commands parent command.

methods.name: ParseFlags
methods.doc: ParseFlags parses persistent flag tree and local flags.

methods.name: PersistentFlags
methods.doc: PersistentFlags returns the persistent FlagSet specifically set in the current command.

methods.name: Print
methods.doc: Print is a convenience method to Print to the defined output, fallback to Stderr if not set.

methods.name: PrintErr
methods.doc: PrintErr is a convenience method to Print to the defined Err output, fallback to Stderr if not set.

methods.name: PrintErrf
methods.doc: PrintErrf is a convenience method to Printf to the defined Err output, fallback to Stderr if not set.

methods.name: PrintErrln
methods.doc: PrintErrln is a convenience method to Println to the defined Err output, fallback to Stderr if not set.

methods.name: Printf
methods.doc: Printf is a convenience method to Printf to the defined output, fallback to Stderr if not set.

methods.name: Println
methods.doc: Println is a convenience method to Println to the defined output, fallback to Stderr if not set.

methods.name: RegisterFlagCompletionFunc
methods.doc:
RegisterFlagCompletionFunc should be called to register a function to provide completion for a flag.

You can use pre-defined completion functions such as [FixedCompletions] or [NoFileCompletions],
or you can define your own.
methods.signature: func  RegisterFlagCompletionFunc(flagName string, f CompletionFunc) error

methods.name: RemoveCommand
methods.doc: RemoveCommand removes one or more commands from a parent command.

methods.name: ResetCommands
methods.doc: ResetCommands delete parent, subcommand and help command from c.

methods.name: ResetFlags
methods.doc: ResetFlags deletes all flags from command.

methods.name: Root
methods.doc: Root finds root command.

methods.name: Runnable
methods.doc: Runnable determines if the command is itself runnable.

methods.name: SetArgs
methods.doc:
SetArgs sets arguments for the command. It is set to os.Args[1:] by default, if desired, can be overridden
particularly useful when testing.

methods.name: SetCompletionCommandGroupID
methods.doc: SetCompletionCommandGroupID sets the group id of the completion command.

methods.name: SetContext
methods.doc:
SetContext sets context for the command. This context will be overwritten by
Command.ExecuteContext or Command.ExecuteContextC.

methods.name: SetErr
methods.doc:
SetErr sets the destination for error messages.
If newErr is nil, os.Stderr is used.

methods.name: SetErrPrefix
methods.doc: SetErrPrefix sets error message prefix to be used. Application can use it to set custom prefix.

methods.name: SetFlagErrorFunc
methods.doc:
SetFlagErrorFunc sets a function to generate an error when flag parsing
fails.

methods.name: SetGlobalNormalizationFunc
methods.doc:
SetGlobalNormalizationFunc sets a normalization function to all flag sets and also to child commands.
The user should not have a cyclic dependency on commands.
methods.signature: func  SetGlobalNormalizationFunc(n func(f *flag.FlagSet, name string) flag.NormalizedName)

methods.name: SetHelpCommand
methods.doc: SetHelpCommand sets help command.

methods.name: SetHelpCommandGroupID
methods.doc: SetHelpCommandGroupID sets the group id of the help command.

methods.name: SetHelpFunc
methods.doc: SetHelpFunc sets help function. Can be defined by Application.

methods.name: SetHelpTemplate
methods.doc: SetHelpTemplate sets help template to be used. Application can use it to set custom template.

methods.name: SetIn
methods.doc:
SetIn sets the source for input data
If newIn is nil, os.Stdin is used.

methods.name: SetOut
methods.doc:
SetOut sets the destination for usage messages.
If newOut is nil, os.Stdout is used.

methods.name: SetOutput
methods.doc:
SetOutput sets the destination for usage and error messages.
If output is nil, os.Stderr is used.

Deprecated: Use SetOut and/or SetErr instead

methods.name: SetUsageFunc
methods.doc: SetUsageFunc sets usage function. Usage can be defined by application.

methods.name: SetUsageTemplate
methods.doc: SetUsageTemplate sets usage template. Can be defined by Application.

methods.name: SetVersionTemplate
methods.doc: SetVersionTemplate sets version template to be used. Application can use it to set custom template.

methods.name: SuggestionsFor
methods.doc: SuggestionsFor provides suggestions for the typedName.

methods.name: Traverse
methods.doc:
Traverse the command tree to find the command, and parse args for
each parent.

methods.name: Usage
methods.doc:
Usage puts out the usage for the command.
Used when a user provides invalid input.
Can be defined by user by overriding UsageFunc.

methods.name: UsageFunc
methods.doc:
UsageFunc returns either the function set by SetUsageFunc for this command
or a parent, or it returns a default usage function.

methods.name: UsagePadding
methods.doc: UsagePadding return padding for the usage.

methods.name: UsageString
methods.doc: UsageString returns usage string.

methods.name: UsageTemplate
methods.doc:
UsageTemplate returns usage template for the command.
This function is kept for backwards-compatibility reasons.

methods.name: UseLine
methods.doc: UseLine puts out the full usage for a given command (including parents).

methods.name: ValidateFlagGroups
methods.doc:
ValidateFlagGroups validates the mutuallyExclusive/oneRequired/requiredAsGroup logic and returns the
first error encountered.

methods.name: ValidateRequiredFlags
methods.doc: ValidateRequiredFlags validates all required flags are present and returns an error otherwise

methods.name: VersionTemplate
methods.doc:
VersionTemplate return version template for the command.
This function is kept for backwards-compatibility reasons.

methods.name: VisitParents
methods.doc: VisitParents visits all parents of the command and invokes fn on each parent.

types.name: Completion
types.doc:
Completion is a string that can be used for completions

two formats are supported:
  - the completion choice
  - the completion choice with a textual description (separated by a TAB).

[CompletionWithDesc] can be used to create a completion string with a textual description.

Note: Go type alias is used to provide a more descriptive name in the documentation, but any string can be used.

types.name: CompletionFunc
types.doc: CompletionFunc is a function that provides completion results.
types.declaration: type CompletionFunc = func(cmd *Command, args []string, toComplete string) ([]Completion, ShellCompDirective)

types.name: CompletionOptions
types.doc: CompletionOptions are the options to control shell completion

fields.name: DisableDefaultCmd
fields.doc: DisableDefaultCmd prevents Cobra from creating a default 'completion' command

fields.name: DisableNoDescFlag
fields.doc:
DisableNoDescFlag prevents Cobra from creating the '--no-descriptions' flag
for shells that support completion descriptions

fields.name: DisableDescriptions
fields.doc:
DisableDescriptions turns off all completion descriptions for shells
that support them

fields.name: HiddenDefaultCmd
fields.doc: HiddenDefaultCmd makes the default 'completion' command hidden

fields.name: DefaultShellCompDirective
fields.doc:
DefaultShellCompDirective sets the ShellCompDirective that is returned
if no special directive can be determined

types.name: FParseErrWhitelist
types.doc: FParseErrWhitelist configures Flag parse errors to be ignored

types.name: Group
types.doc: Group Structure to manage groups for commands

types.name: ShellCompDirective
types.doc:
ShellCompDirective is a bit map representing the different behaviors the shell
can be instructed to have once completions have been provided.

types.name: SliceValue
types.doc:
SliceValue is a reduced version of [pflag.SliceValue]. It is used to detect
flags that accept multiple values and therefore can provide completion
multiple times.

fields.name: GetSlice
fields.doc: GetSlice returns the flag value list as an array of strings.

funcs.name: GenMan
funcs.doc:
GenMan will generate a man page for the given command and write it to
w. The header argument may be nil, however obviously w may not.
funcs.signature: func GenMan(cmd *cobra.Command, header *GenManHeader, w io.Writer) error

examples.name: GenMan

funcs.name: GenManTree
funcs.doc:
GenManTree will generate a man page for this command and all descendants
in the directory given. The header may be nil. This function may not work
correctly if your command names have `-` in them. If you have `cmd` with two
subcmds, `sub` and `sub-third`, and `sub` has a subcommand called `third`
it is undefined which help output will be in the file `cmd-sub-third.1`.
funcs.signature: func GenManTree(cmd *cobra.Command, header *GenManHeader, dir string) error

examples.name: GenManTree

funcs.name: GenManTreeFromOpts
funcs.doc:
GenManTreeFromOpts generates a man page for the command and all descendants.
The pages are written to the opts.Path directory.

funcs.name: GenMarkdown
funcs.doc: GenMarkdown creates markdown output.

funcs.name: GenMarkdownCustom
funcs.doc: GenMarkdownCustom creates custom markdown output.
funcs.signature: func GenMarkdownCustom(cmd *cobra.Command, w io.Writer, linkHandler func(string) string) error

funcs.name: GenMarkdownTree
funcs.doc:
GenMarkdownTree will generate a markdown page for this command and all
descendants in the directory given. The header may be nil.
This function may not work correctly if your command names have `-` in them.
If you have `cmd` with two subcmds, `sub` and `sub-third`,
and `sub` has a subcommand called `third`, it is undefined which
help output will be in the file `cmd-sub-third.1`.

funcs.name: GenMarkdownTreeCustom
funcs.doc:
GenMarkdownTreeCustom is the same as GenMarkdownTree, but
with custom filePrepender and linkHandler.
funcs.signature: func GenMarkdownTreeCustom(cmd *cobra.Command, dir string, filePrepender, linkHandler func(string) string) error

funcs.name: GenReST
funcs.doc: GenReST creates reStructured Text output.

funcs.name: GenReSTCustom
funcs.doc: GenReSTCustom creates custom reStructured Text output.
funcs.signature: func GenReSTCustom(cmd *cobra.Command, w io.Writer, linkHandler func(string, string) string) error

funcs.name: GenReSTTree
funcs.doc:
GenReSTTree will generate a ReST page for this command and all
descendants in the directory given.
This function may not work correctly if your command names have `-` in them.
If you have `cmd` with two subcmds, `sub` and `sub-third`,
and `sub` has a subcommand called `third`, it is undefined which
help output will be in the file `cmd-sub-third.1`.

funcs.name: GenReSTTreeCustom
funcs.doc:
GenReSTTreeCustom is the same as GenReSTTree, but
with custom filePrepender and linkHandler.
funcs.signature: func GenReSTTreeCustom(cmd *cobra.Command, dir string, filePrepender func(string) string, linkHandler func(string, string) string) error

funcs.name: GenYaml
funcs.doc: GenYaml creates yaml output.

funcs.name: GenYamlCustom
funcs.doc: GenYamlCustom creates custom yaml output.
funcs.signature: func GenYamlCustom(cmd *cobra.Command, w io.Writer, linkHandler func(string) string) error

funcs.name: GenYamlTree
funcs.doc:
GenYamlTree creates yaml structured ref files for this command and all descendants
in the directory given. This function may not work
correctly if your command names have `-` in them. If you have `cmd` with two
subcmds, `sub` and `sub-third`, and `sub` has a subcommand called `third`
it is undefined which help output will be in the file `cmd-sub-third.1`.

funcs.name: GenYamlTreeCustom
funcs.doc: GenYamlTreeCustom creates yaml structured ref files.
funcs.signature: func GenYamlTreeCustom(cmd *cobra.Command, dir string, filePrepender, linkHandler func(string) string) error

types.name: GenManHeader
types.doc:
GenManHeader is a lot like the .TH header at the start of man pages. These
include the title, section, date, source, and manual. We will use the
current time if Date is unset and will use "Auto generated by spf13/cobra"
if the Source is unset.

types.name: GenManTreeOptions
types.doc:
GenManTreeOptions is the options for generating the man pages.
Used only in GenManTreeFromOpts.
